_comment_mission_metadata: This section defines the mission's core identity and requirements
_comment_mission_id: Unique identifier. Format: lowercase_with_underscores. Must be unique across all missions.
title: The Abandoned Warehouse
_comment_title: Player-facing mission name. Should be evocative and hint at content without spoiling it.
_comment_type: Mission type determines structure and rewards. Options: salvage, exploration, trade, rescue, combat, story
_comment_location: Where the mission takes place. Helps establish atmosphere and world-building.
description: A pre-exodus industrial warehouse still has functioning power. Sensors indicate valuable ship parts inside, but automated security may still be active.
_comment_description: Mission briefing text. 2-3 sentences. Sets up the situation and stakes.
_comment_difficulty: 1-5 stars. Affects rewards and expected player level. 1=Tutorial/Easy, 2=Easy, 3=Medium, 4=Hard, 5=Very Hard
_comment_requirements: What the player needs to access this mission

requirements._comment_min_level: Minimum player level required. 1=no requirement, 2-5=gated content
requirements._comment_required_systems: Array of system names required at Level 1+. Example: ['sensors', 'computer']
requirements._comment_completed_missions: Array of mission_ids that must be completed first. Creates mission chains.

_comment_objectives: Mission goals shown to player in mission UI
objectives: Retrieve ship parts from the warehouse
_comment_objectives_format: First objective is always primary goal. Additional objectives marked [OPTIONAL] provide bonus rewards.
_comment_stages: Mission stages represent major decision points. Salvage missions typically have 3-4 stages: Approach, Navigate, Retrieve, Escape

stages._comment_stage_approach: First stage: How does the player approach the situation?
stages.title: Approaching the Warehouse
stages.description: The warehouse looms ahead, solar panels still collecting energy after decades. Security drones patrol the perimeter in lazy circles. There's a main entrance, and your sensors detect a maintenance shaft on the east side.
stages._comment_image: Asset reference for this stage. Format: asset_name (no extension)

choices._comment_choice_id: Unique within this stage. Format: action_descriptor
choices.text: Enter through the front door—direct approach
choices._comment_text: Choice button text. Should be clear and action-oriented.
choices._comment_requirements: If empty, choice is always available. Can require: skill (engineering/diplomacy/combat/science) + skill_level (0-10)
choices._comment_success_chance: 100 = always succeeds, skill_based = roll against skill level, 0-99 = fixed percentage

success.text: The front door opens to a dusty lobby. Security terminal is right there, but it's active. The drones know you're here.
success._comment_next_stage: stage_id of the next stage to go to
success._comment_effects: Array of effect flags. Used in conditional_text and rewards_modified. Format: lowercase_with_underscores
success._comment_xp_bonus: Additional XP for this choice. Base mission XP is in rewards section.

success.text: The shaft is tight and dusty, but you emerge in the storage area undetected. The parts are right ahead.

consequences._comment_failure: If success_chance < 100, include a failure consequence

choices._comment_requirements_skill: Skill checks: engineering, diplomacy, combat, science. skill_level 0-10 determines difficulty.
choices._comment_success_chance_skill: skill_based means the game calculates success based on player's skill level

success.text: You tap into the warehouse network. Security drones power down. The facility is yours.

failure.text: The security AI detects your intrusion attempt. Alarms blare. Multiple drones converge on your position.

stages._comment_stage_navigate: Second stage: Dealing with challenges inside
stages.title: Inside the Warehouse
stages.description: Rows of storage crates stretch into darkness. Your scanner leads you to the parts section. Hull plates, power cells, and thruster components—jackpot. But one crate is sealed with a biometric lock.

choices.text: Take what you can access and leave quietly

success.text: You gather accessible parts: hull plates and a power cell. Not a huge haul, but you're in and out clean.
success._comment_mission_result: Use 'success' or 'failure' to mark mission completion

choices._comment_success_chance_percentage: 70% chance means 30% chance of failure. Used for risky actions.

success.text: The crate pops open, revealing rare thruster components. Worth the risk.

failure.text: The crate's alarm screams. Security drones activate. Time to run!

choices.text: Bypass the biometric lock with engineering

success.text: You trick the lock into accepting your biometric signature. Inside: high-quality thruster components. The best salvage you've seen in weeks.

failure.text: The lock rejects your attempt and triggers security. Drones incoming!

stages.title: Clean Exit
stages.description: Parts secured, no alarms triggered. You head for the exit, job well done.

success.text: You slip out the way you came. Another successful salvage run. These parts will keep you flying.

stages.title: Mission Complete
stages.description: Salvage successful. Parts acquired. Time to get back to work.

_comment_rewards: Base rewards for completing the mission successfully

rewards._comment_xp: Base XP. Difficulty 2 salvage = 100-150 XP. Bonuses from choices add to this.
rewards._comment_credits: Currency reward. Difficulty 2 = 200-400 credits. Used to purchase parts.
rewards._comment_items: Physical parts added to inventory. Format: {part_id: valid_part_id, quantity: positive_int}. See PartRegistry for valid IDs.
rewards._comment_discovered_parts: Parts unlocked in workshop catalog. Player can now find/buy these. Can overlap with items (you get the part AND unlock it for future).
rewards._comment_unlocks: Mission IDs unlocked by completing this mission. Creates quest chains and progression gates.

_comment_conditional_rewards: Rewards that depend on player choices (effects flags)

if_got_rare_parts._comment: If player forced open the locked crate or bypassed the biometric lock

if_silent_entry._comment: If player completed mission without triggering alarms

_comment_failure_consequences: What happens if the mission fails

failure_consequences._comment_failure_xp: Even failure provides some XP (learning experience). Typically 25-50 XP.
failure_consequences._comment_failure_effects: Story flags for failed missions. Can affect future content.

_design_philosophy: Salvage missions focus on risk/reward decisions. Skilled players can get better outcomes, but even cautious play succeeds.
_stage_structure: Approach (3 choices) -> Navigate (3 choices) -> Escape (1-2 choices). Total playtime: 5-10 minutes.
_skill_checks: Engineering 3 (accessible) and Engineering 4 (challenging). Rewards scale with difficulty.
_reward_balance: Base 150 XP + 400 credits = fair for difficulty 2. Bonuses can add 65-105 XP for perfect play.
_part_rewards: 2 common parts guaranteed. 1 uncommon part for skilled/lucky play. Follows rarity distribution.
_replayability: Multiple paths, different outcomes. Players can retry for better rewards or different story beats.
